claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand your situation. 

**First, let me ask about your child:**

How old is the child (or children) you're asking about? This will help determine the eligibility pathway:

- **Under 16**: Children under 16 generally qualify automatically
- **16-19**: Young people aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me the age(s) of your child/children.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello, yes I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me help you determine eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, who is 13 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, they fall into the automatic qualification age category. However, I need to check a few more things to confirm eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to check a few eligibility criteria:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

This is a standard check to ensure the child qualifies. For a 13-year-old, this would be unusual, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 13!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in Alex's own name (not as part of your household claim). 

For a 13-year-old, this would also be quite unusual. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any benefits in their own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

This is relevant because certain types of apprenticeships can affect Child Benefit eligibility. The rules vary by region:
- In England: All apprenticeships would disqualify
- In Scotland: Modern Apprenticeships would disqualify
- In Wales: Most apprenticeships disqualify, except Foundation Apprenticeships which are approved

Given that Alex is 13, I'm assuming they're not in an apprenticeship, but can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - they're still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check your residency status, as this is an important eligibility requirement.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (most common)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these situations apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm afraid none of those apply to me. I don't currently live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Unfortunately, this means you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

### Why You're Not Eligible:

While Alex meets all the child-related requirements (being 13 years old, not working full-time, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**, which is a mandatory condition for claiming Child Benefit.

### Residency Requirements:

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- **Normally live in the UK**, OR
- Meet one of the special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria like working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
  - Being a Crown servant living abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you don't currently live in the UK and don't meet any of the special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What You Can Do:

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move to the UK or establish UK residency), you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

If you have any questions about your specific situation or need further guidance, you can contact HMRC's Child Benefit helpline or visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I hope this clarifies your situation. Please let me know if you have any other questions.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and meets the basic child criteria: not working 24+ hours per week, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances such as having settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria (working, studying, or having sufficient resources), being a Crown servant abroad, or living in certain qualifying countries. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not meet any of these special circumstances, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant fails to meet the UK residency requirements, which is a mandatory condition for claiming Child Benefit. Without normal UK residence or qualifying special circumstances, the claim cannot proceed.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
